name: Steamed Apple-Date Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a deep 2 1/2-quart heat-safe bowl heavily. Cut foil 1 inch larger than the top of the bowl to use as cover. Grease one side of the foil well and set aside.
2. Blend dates and apple cider in a blender at medium speed or food processor with knife blade attached until smooth. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat sugar and butter or margarine just until blended. Increase speed to high; beat until light and fluffy. Add eggs and the remaining ingredients, beating on low speed until blended. Increase speed to medium; beat 1 minute, scraping the bowl occasionally with a rubber spatula. Gently fold in chopped apple and walnuts. Pour the pudding mixture into the prepared bowl. Cover the bowl with foil, greased side down, and tie tightly with string.
4. Place the bowl on a rack in a 8-12 quart saucepan. Pour water halfway up the side of the bowl, touching the foil. Heat over low heat; cover and simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
5. About 30 minutes before the pudding is done, prepare the Custard Sauce.
6. When the pudding is done, let it cool in the bowl on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Loosen the pudding with a metal spatula and invert onto a warm platter. Garnish with red and green candied cherries. Serve the pudding warm with the warm sauce.
Custard Sauce:
1. In a heavy 2-quart saucepan over low heat or in a double boiler over hot, not boiling, water, beat egg yolks, sugar, and salt with a wire whisk until thick and coats the back of a spoon, about 25 minutes. Stir in vanilla extract. Serve warm or cold over apple pie, fruitcake, cut-up fruit, or ice cream.
